title: Meisneria cordata
intro: Meisneria cordata is a perennial plant species that belongs to the family Apocynaceae, commonly known as the dogbane or oleander family. Traditionally, Meisneria cordata has been used in herbal medicine to treat various health conditions, including inflammation, fever, and respiratory issues, due to its potential anti-inflammatory, antipyretic, and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Meisneria cordata include alkaloids, flavonoids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its therapeutic effects. In herbal medicine, Meisneria cordata is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, and it is also used to make tinctures and extracts, which are used to treat a range of health conditions, including coughs, colds, and bronchitis.
